message:
feat(model,tui): expand data model and TUI rendering with analysis views

- model.rs: +43 lines adding structured data model types for analysis
  output and export formatting
- tui.rs: +93 lines extending TUI with table views, scrolling, and
  detail panel rendering
- Rotate 4 stale beads history snapshots and sync tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -33,7 +33,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","title":"Define the evidence standard for parity claims so beads can be closed honestly","description":"Purpose:
Define what counts as sufficient evidence for saying a surface is done. Some parity claims can be established by fixture-backed conformance; others need TUI journey coverage, shell e2e, or targeted regression tests.

Acceptance criteria:
- Different evidence classes are spelled out for robot, TUI, export/pages, and workspace/hook surfaces.
- The plan distinguishes proof of implementation from proof of behavioral equivalence.
- Closing guidance is strict enough that we do not recreate the current overclaim problem.","notes":"Parity evidence standard:

A. General rule
- A parity claim is valid only when the tracker can point to both the implementation anchor and the proof anchor.
- "Implemented" is not enough. We distinguish: implemented, proven equivalent, partially proven, divergent-by-design, and unknown.
- If the behavior affects operator recovery, path resolution, or cross-mode/context continuity, pure happy-path testing is insufficient.

B. Evidence ladder for loader/model/path semantics
- Required minimum: focused unit tests for parsing/validation rules plus CLI-entry integration coverage for cwd/repo/workspace/beads-file resolution semantics.
- Needed when legacy behavior is subtle: adversarial fixtures or integration cases that exercise malformed input, missing files, ambiguous roots, and precedence rules.
- Closure bar: a future agent can tell which root/path semantics are guaranteed and which are merely inferred.

C. Evidence ladder for robot/CLI contract parity
- Required minimum: one deterministic proof layer near the implementation (unit/conformance/schema) and one proof layer from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Use conformance fixtures when legacy output shape/order/omission is the source of truth.
- Use schema validation when shape contracts matter beyond a single fixture.
- Use CLI integration/e2e when path/config resolution, error behavior, or multi-flag interaction matters.
- Closure bar: command family behavior, default behavior, filter behavior, and failure behavior are all evidenced at the right layer.

D. Evidence ladder for TUI workflow parity
- Required minimum: state/keyflow coverage for the interaction logic plus either snapshot or journey evidence for the user-visible workflow.
- Use snapshots when layout/detail visibility matters.
- Use keyflow/state tests when search/filter/focus/selection transitions matter.
- Use multi-mode journey tests when the operator workflow crosses views or depends on sustained context.
- Closure bar: we can explain not just that a key exists, but that the operator can complete the legacy job-to-be-done.

E. Evidence ladder for export/pages/workspace integration parity
- Required minimum: targeted unit/integration coverage for root-cause semantics plus shell/e2e proof for operator workflows with detailed logs.
- Required logging: cwd/repo/workspace context, replay command, stdout/stderr, and any preview/watch lifecycle artifacts relevant to diagnosis.
- Use failure-path cases deliberately: invalid saved config, broken hook, non-repo cwd, watch reload edge cases, preview path traversal protections, and deploy-target repair flows.
- Closure bar: both happy path and operator recovery path are evidenced.

F. Documentation truthfulness standard
- Docs may only claim parity at the strongest evidence level actually achieved.
- Additive Rust-only features must be labeled as additive, not counted as proof that a legacy workflow is covered.
- Intentional divergences must name the preserved user outcome and why the difference is acceptable.

G. Anti-overclaim rule
- A bead cannot close by citing only old markdown prose or broad suite counts. It must name the specific tests, fixtures, scripts, or artifacts that establish confidence for that surface.
- If evidence is missing but the code looks plausible, classify as partial or unknown until proof exists.

H. Diagnostic quality standard
- Tests and scripts used as parity evidence must fail loudly and locally. A future agent should be able to infer the broken workflow, command family, mode, path context, or fixture from the failure output without rereading the implementation.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.806036575Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29.805311652Z","close_reason":"Tracker now records the parity evidence standard, including required proof ladders and the anti-overclaim rule for closing beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","parity","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:42.803904168Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":135,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Evidence standard recorded:
Parity closure now requires paired implementation and proof anchors, with explicit ladders for loader/path semantics, robot CLI contracts, TUI workflows, export/pages/workspace integrations, and documentation truthfulness. The anti-overclaim rule is explicit: no bead closes on broad suite counts or optimistic prose alone.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:42:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","title":"Record the fresh gap ledger with explicit statuses: proven, partial, unknown, divergent-by-design","description":"Purpose:
Translate the inventory and evidence standard into an actionable gap ledger. This is the gate between planning and implementation.

Requirements:
- Every material gap becomes a child bead in one of the execution tracks.
- Every currently claimed-complete surface is either revalidated or downgraded.
- Any intentional divergence must include why it is acceptable and what user outcome remains covered.","notes":"Fresh gap ledger snapshot:

1. Loader/model/root-resolution semantics
- Status: partial / high-risk.
- Why: recent real bugs in feedback storage, hook cwd, preview path handling, wizard repair routing, and repo-path/beads-file inference all came from this family.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

2. Core robot command families (triage/next/plan/priority/insights/diff/history/forecast/capacity/burndown/suggest/graph)
- Status: implemented and broadly proven, but not yet re-audited under the new evidence standard. Treat as provisionally partial until command-by-command audit confirms defaults, filters, omission rules, and failure behavior.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.4.2`.

3. Extended/additive robot surfaces (search, label intel, recipes, docs/schema, metrics, feedback, drift, correlation/impact, file-intel, briefs, sprint helpers)
- Status: mixed. Some are additive Rust improvements rather than strict legacy surfaces; others are claimed complete in docs but need truthfulness review so additive does not get confused with parity proof.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.5.1`.

4. Main TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at operator-workflow level. Existing snapshots and keybindings are not enough by themselves to prove users can complete the same browsing, prioritization, and detail-inspection tasks as in legacy `bv`.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

5. Board/insights/graph TUI workflow parity
- Status: partial / unproven at workflow level. The modes are rich and heavily tested, but the new standard requires proof of operator jobs-to-be-done rather than mode existence or keybinding coverage.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

6. History-mode and cross-mode investigative parity
- Status: partial / high-value audit target. History is specifically vulnerable to overclaim because individual features can exist without the full investigative loop feeling complete.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.4.3`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.5.2`.

7. Export/pages/preview/watch/wizard workflows
- Status: implemented and actively tested, but still provisional under the new standard because happy-path coverage and feature presence do not automatically prove workflow equivalence or recovery-path clarity.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.4.2`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.5.2`.

8. Documentation and release-readiness claims
- Status: incorrect/overstated in current form. README and FEATURE_PARITY contain strong completion language that now must be treated as claims to be re-earned, not evidence.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1`, `bd-2bpl.5.2`.

9. Divergent-by-design/additive surfaces
- Status: divergent-by-design where clearly Rust-specific and not required for legacy parity, but must be labeled honestly. Examples: Rust-only TUI modes, schema/docs outputs, some metrics/reporting differences.
- Existing execution beads covering it: `bd-2bpl.5.1` plus whatever audit bead owns the adjacent workflow family.

Planning conclusion
- No additional top-level tracks are needed right now; the current `bd-2bpl.2` through `.5` split is sufficient.
- The first implementation-phase priorities should stay audit-first: `bd-2bpl.2.1`, `bd-2bpl.2.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.1`, `bd-2bpl.3.2`, `bd-2bpl.3.3`, `bd-2bpl.4.1`, `bd-2bpl.4.2`, and `bd-2bpl.4.3`.
- The strongest current downgrade is this: broad implementation exists, but exact capability parity is not yet proven under the new evidence standard.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.151000528Z","closed_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:08.150434155Z","close_reason":"Fresh gap ledger recorded in tracker notes/comments, with provisional statuses and explicit mapping from each gap family to the current execution beads","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","gaps","parity"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.016828423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:21.706308818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:22.959815646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":136,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Gap ledger recorded:
The tracker now explicitly downgrades several surfaces from implicit "done" to provisional partial status until fresh audit evidence exists. The biggest current risk classes are path/root-resolution semantics, operator-workflow proof for the TUI, export/pages recovery-path behavior, and documentation truthfulness. No new top-level tracks are needed; the current execution tree is sufficient.","created_at":"2026-03-14T03:43:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2","title":"Audit and close robot/CLI parity gaps against legacy bv semantics","description":"Background:
Robot-mode parity is the most objective and automation-critical surface. Even small path-resolution, filtering, metadata-shape, or loader-semantic mismatches can break downstream agent workflows.

Goal:
Systematically re-audit all legacy robot and CLI behaviors against the Rust port, then fix any remaining gaps in semantics, outputs, or workflow compatibility.

Acceptance:
- Loader, model, and path-resolution semantics are rechecked against legacy behavior.
- Every legacy robot command family has explicit parity evidence or a documented intentional divergence.
- Integration and conformance coverage exists for all reopened gaps.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.077749232Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.802710388Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","title":"Re-audit loader, model, and path-resolution semantics against legacy behavior","description":"Purpose:
Audit the subtle compatibility layer that everything else depends on: `.beads` discovery, workspace/repo-path selection, warning suppression in robot mode, JSONL file precedence, BOM handling, malformed-line behavior, model validation, and status/dependency semantics.

Why this matters:
Many operator-visible parity problems are really path or loader mismatches in disguise. This bead should flush out those root causes before they continue leaking into downstream surfaces.

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58.590328804Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: audited loader and path-resolution semantics against legacy worktree expectations and found a real parity gap in .beads discovery for linked git worktrees. Root cause: get_beads_dir() only searched ancestor .beads directories and did not fall back from a worktree .git pointer to the main repo .beads; the first implementation of that fallback also needed canonicalization of commondir to resolve dot-dot segments correctly. Fix landed in src/loader.rs: parse worktree .git, resolve gitdir, canonicalize commondir, derive the main repo root, and use the main-repo .beads when present. Added regression test get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree passed; prior rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets on this slice were green. cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide due existing unrelated drift in other files, but src/loader.rs was formatted with rustfmt --edition 2024.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:24:04Z"},{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: hardened the linked-worktree fallback in src/loader.rs after review identified an ancestor-scan correctness hole. Root cause: find_worktree_main_repo_beads_dir_from() used early-return option propagation inside the ancestor loop, so any malformed or non-worktree .git file in an intermediate ancestor aborted the entire scan before reaching the real worktree root. Fix: treat each ancestor independently, continue past invalid .git pointers or unusable commondir data, and only return when a valid main-repo .beads directory is found. Added regression test get_beads_dir_ignores_malformed_intermediate_git_file_during_worktree_fallback alongside the existing worktree fallback tests. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_ passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","title":"Re-audit loader, model, and path-resolution semantics against legacy behavior","description":"Purpose:
Audit the subtle compatibility layer that everything else depends on: `.beads` discovery, workspace/repo-path selection, warning suppression in robot mode, JSONL file precedence, BOM handling, malformed-line behavior, model validation, and status/dependency semantics.

Why this matters:
Many operator-visible parity problems are really path or loader mismatches in disguise. This bead should flush out those root causes before they continue leaking into downstream surfaces.

Done means:
There is a concrete list of matching semantics, mismatches, and required fixes/tests.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T20:47:34.978837950Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:24.264893609Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.254947617Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":137,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: audited loader and path-resolution semantics against legacy worktree expectations and found a real parity gap in .beads discovery for linked git worktrees. Root cause: get_beads_dir() only searched ancestor .beads directories and did not fall back from a worktree .git pointer to the main repo .beads; the first implementation of that fallback also needed canonicalization of commondir to resolve dot-dot segments correctly. Fix landed in src/loader.rs: parse worktree .git, resolve gitdir, canonicalize commondir, derive the main repo root, and use the main-repo .beads when present. Added regression test get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_falls_back_to_main_repo_for_git_worktree passed; prior rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets and rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets on this slice were green. cargo fmt --check remains red repo-wide due existing unrelated drift in other files, but src/loader.rs was formatted with rustfmt --edition 2024.","created_at":"2026-03-14T04:24:04Z"},{"id":138,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: hardened the linked-worktree fallback in src/loader.rs after review identified an ancestor-scan correctness hole. Root cause: find_worktree_main_repo_beads_dir_from() used early-return option propagation inside the ancestor loop, so any malformed or non-worktree .git file in an intermediate ancestor aborted the entire scan before reaching the real worktree root. Fix: treat each ancestor independently, continue past invalid .git pointers or unusable commondir data, and only return when a valid main-repo .beads directory is found. Added regression test get_beads_dir_ignores_malformed_intermediate_git_file_during_worktree_fallback alongside the existing worktree fallback tests. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib get_beads_dir_ passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails on unrelated pre-existing drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T18:42:58Z"},{"id":139,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","author":"ubuntu","text":"Progress update: the audit found a model-level validation gap in src/model.rs. Legacy validation requires updated_at to be greater than or equal to created_at when both timestamps are present, but Issue::validate() was not checking that invariant at all. Fix: reject issues whose updated_at is earlier than created_at while still accepting equal timestamps. Added direct unit regressions validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at and validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps. Verification: export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_rejects_updated_at_before_created_at passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo test --lib validate_accepts_equal_created_and_updated_timestamps passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo check --all-targets passed; export TMPDIR=/data/tmp && rch exec -- cargo clippy --all-targets passed with the repo existing warning set. cargo fmt --check still fails only because of unrelated pre-existing formatting drift in other files outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-14T20:47:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","title":"Re-audit each robot command family for behavioral and output-shape equivalence","description":"Purpose:
Review every legacy robot family again with fresh eyes: triage, next, plan, insights, priority, diff, history, forecast, capacity, burndown, suggest, graph, search, label intelligence, recipes/scripts, docs/schema, and admin or operational flags.

Focus:
- Field shape and omission rules.
- Filter semantics and default behavior.
- Path/config resolution.
- Error/warning behavior and deterministic ordering.

Done means:
Every reopened mismatch becomes either a fix bead or a justified divergence note.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T04:45:26.862305130Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:25.154059909Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.487822114Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","title":"Close reopened robot/CLI mismatches discovered by the fresh audit","description":"Purpose:
This is the implementation bucket for concrete robot/CLI bugs exposed by the fresh audit. Do not close it based on planning alone. Populate it via child comments or follow-up beads that reference the exact mismatch and root cause.

Acceptance:
- Every fix includes targeted regression coverage.
- New path/config behaviors are covered from the CLI entrypoint, not only internal helpers.
- The final state preserves determinism and legacy-compatible defaults unless a divergence is explicitly documented.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49.741578308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:43.759806340Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.047404675Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:26.598234256Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":131,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
This is now a coordination container. Actual implementation should usually land on `bd-2bpl.2.3.1` and/or `bd-2bpl.2.3.2`, with each code change tied back to a specific audited mismatch.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:49Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","title":"Fix loader/model/path-resolution mismatches surfaced by the robot audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes for loader, model, and path-resolution mismatches discovered in `bd-2bpl.2.1`. Keep this bead focused on discovery/root-cause classes that affect all downstream robot workflows, not on per-command payload details.

Required scope:
- `.beads` and workspace discovery semantics.
- `--repo-path`, `--workspace`, and `--beads-file` precedence and project-root inference.
- JSONL precedence, BOM/malformed-line handling, warning suppression, and validation semantics.
- Model/status/dependency interpretation differences that can change graph shape or command output.

Acceptance:
- Root-cause fixes are implemented without reducing existing functionality.
- Regressions are covered with targeted unit and CLI-entry integration tests.
- Any intentional divergence is recorded explicitly in comments or follow-up beads.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.140207150Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","loader","parity","robot"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:29.139341113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.161569700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
@@ -43,7 +43,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","title":"Add robot CLI integration and e2e scripts with detailed diagnostics and artifact capture","description":"Purpose:
Cover whole-command workflows that unit and fixture tests cannot fully validate. Add CLI integration coverage and, where useful, shell/e2e scripts that capture stdout, stderr, replay commands, and fixture context for failures.

Acceptance:
- Reopened robot workflows are exercised from the real CLI entrypoint.
- Diagnostic output is rich enough for a future agent to reproduce failures quickly.
- Scripts and tests remain deterministic and `rch`-friendly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.429726740Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","parity","robot","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:13.794841828Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.2","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.2.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:31.428943569Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3","title":"Audit and close interactive TUI workflow gaps without chasing pixel parity","description":"Background:
The TUI does not need to look exactly like the legacy Go implementation, but it does need to support the same operator workflows, navigation loops, search/discovery behaviors, and task-completion outcomes.

Goal:
Audit core TUI workflows with fresh eyes, identify interaction gaps or regressions, and improve the frankentui implementation until it covers the legacy workflow surface and is at least as usable.

Acceptance:
- Main, board, insights, graph, and history workflows are audited end-to-end.
- Missing or weaker interactions are converted into concrete implementation tasks.
- The resulting TUI is behaviorally equivalent or better for core legacy jobs, even if visual styling differs.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:52.113236988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","tui","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:41.981524477Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":129,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"TUI track guidance:
Judge the Rust TUI by workflow coverage, not by pixel matching. A different layout or nicer presentation is acceptable if operators can still complete the same tasks at least as effectively. Prioritize discovery, navigation, search/filter loops, detail usefulness, and mode transitions.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:43Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","title":"Audit main-view operator workflows and detail-pane usefulness against legacy jobs-to-be-done","description":"Purpose:
Audit the default TUI surface as an operator tool rather than as a rendering artifact. The question is whether a user can browse, prioritize, inspect blockers, search, sort, and decide what to do next as effectively as in legacy `bv`.

Focus:
Selection behavior, detail richness, search loops, filter transitions, sort order expectations, focus changes, and empty/no-hit states.

Outcome:
Produce a concrete list of workflow gaps and fixes, not aesthetic opinions.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T18:41:24.325854862Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["main-view","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:27.769527948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.345527212Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","title":"Audit history mode and cross-mode navigation loops against real operator tasks","description":"Purpose:
History mode is easy to overclaim because individual features can exist without the whole operator workflow feeling complete. Audit commit/bead switching, file tree interaction, search modes, confidence filters, jump-back behavior, and cross-mode transitions.

Acceptance:
The bead documents whether history mode truly covers the legacy investigative workflows or just approximates them.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.968232529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:28.935465379Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["history","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:28.934607739Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:44.968232529Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","title":"Implement the highest-value TUI workflow fixes found by the audit","description":"Purpose:
Implementation bucket for actual interaction fixes. This should absorb concrete issues uncovered by the three audit tasks and translate them into targeted code/test changes.

Guardrails:
- Better-than-legacy visuals are welcome.
- Pixel matching is not required.
- Workflow regressions are not acceptable.
- Changes must preserve deterministic testability through snapshots, state tests, or keyflow journeys.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.279441833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50.229347340Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:39:45.279441833Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:29.552886048Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:30.171090528Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:40:30.804763670Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":132,"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Planning refinement:
This is now a coordination container. Prefer claiming the specific mode-focused child bead (`.1`, `.2`, `.3`) that matches the audited workflow gap. Visual improvements are encouraged only when they preserve or improve operator workflow coverage.","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.1","title":"Implement main-view workflow and detail-pane fixes surfaced by the TUI audit","description":"Purpose:
Implement concrete fixes from `bd-2bpl.3.1` for the default TUI workflow: browsing, selecting, searching, sorting, filtering, focus changes, and detail comprehension.

User bar:
The result should let an operator make decisions at least as effectively as in legacy `bv`, and preferably more effectively. Better information scent and clearer detail are welcome as long as no legacy workflow is lost.

Acceptance:
- Main-view workflow regressions are fixed without narrowing functionality.
- Detail pane improvements are evaluated by operator usefulness, not visual mimicry alone.
- Changes are testable with deterministic state, keyflow, or snapshot coverage.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:14.023723339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:32.038597989Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["implementation","main-view","parity","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:49:32.037808597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4.1","depends_on_id":"bd-2bpl.3.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T02:48:14.023723339Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
